papers.title_en: Self-Evolving Multi-Agent Framework for Efficient Decision Making in Real-Time Strategy Scenarios
papers.authors: Li Ma, Hao Peng, Yiming Wang, Hongbin Luo, Jie Liu, Kongjing Gu, Guanlin Wu, Hui Lin, Lei Ren
papers.evidence: 针对实时场景的LLM低延迟决策框架
papers.tldr: 针对大语言模型在实时策略（RTS）场景中面临的推理延迟高和逻辑一致性差的问题，本文提出了SEMA（自我进化多智能体）框架。该框架通过自适应校准模型偏差、基于结构熵的动态观测剪枝以及混合知识记忆机制，实现了高效、低延迟的决策。在星际争霸II中的实验表明，SEMA在显著提升胜率的同时，将平均决策延迟降低了50%以上，验证了其在复杂动态环境下的鲁棒性。
papers.abstract_en: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated exceptional potential in complex reasoning,pioneering a new paradigm for autonomous agent decision making in dynamic settings. However, in Real-Time Strategy (RTS) scenarios, LLMs suffer from a critical speed-quality trade-off. Specifically expansive state spaces and time limits render inference delays prohibitive, while stochastic planning errors undermine logical consistency. To address these challenges, we present SEMA (Self-Evolving Multi-Agent), a novel framework designed for high-performance, low-latency decision-making in RTS environments. This collaborative multi-agent framework facilitates self-evolution by adaptively calibrating model bias through in-episode assessment and cross-episode analysis. We further incorporate dynamic observation pruning based on structural entropy to model game states topologically. By distilling high dimensional data into core semantic information, this approach significantly reduces inference time. We also develop a hybrid knowledge-memory mechanism that integrates micro-trajectories, macro-experience, and hierarchical domain knowledge, thereby enhancing both strategic adaptability and decision consistency. Experiments across multiple StarCraft II maps demonstrate that SEMA achieves superior win rates while reducing average decision latency by over 50%, validating its efficiency and robustness in complex RTS scenarios.

papers.title_en: AVO: Agentic Variation Operators for Autonomous Evolutionary Search
papers.authors: Terry Chen, Zhifan Ye, Bing Xu, Zihao Ye, Timmy Liu, Ali Hassani, Tianqi Chen, Andrew Kerr, Haicheng Wu, Yang Xu, Yu-Jung Chen, Hanfeng Chen, Aditya Kane, Ronny Krashinsky, Ming-Yu Liu, Vinod Grover, Luis Ceze, Roger Bringmann, John Tran, Wei Liu, Fung Xie, Michael Lightstone, Humphrey Shi
papers.evidence: 自主演化优化注意力机制算子
papers.tldr: 本研究提出代理变异算子（AVO），将传统进化搜索中的固定变异和交叉替换为自主编码代理。AVO利用大模型构建自导向循环，结合领域知识和执行反馈进行代码优化。在NVIDIA B200 GPU上优化注意力机制内核，性能超越cuDNN和FlashAttention-4，展示了AI代理在高性能计算优化中的巨大潜力。
papers.abstract_en: Agentic Variation Operators (AVO) are a new family of evolutionary variation operators that replace the fixed mutation, crossover, and hand-designed heuristics of classical evolutionary search with autonomous coding agents. Rather than confining a language model to candidate generation within a prescribed pipeline, AVO instantiates variation as a self-directed agent loop that can consult the current lineage, a domain-specific knowledge base, and execution feedback to propose, repair, critique, and verify implementation edits. We evaluate AVO on attention, among the most aggressively optimized kernel targets in AI, on NVIDIA Blackwell (B200) GPUs. Over 7 days of continuous autonomous evolution on multi-head attention, AVO discovers kernels that outperform cuDNN by up to 3.5% and FlashAttention-4 by up to 10.5% across the evaluated configurations. The discovered optimizations transfer readily to grouped-query attention, requiring only 30 minutes of additional autonomous adaptation and yielding gains of up to 7.0% over cuDNN and 9.3% over FlashAttention-4. Together, these results show that agentic variation operators move beyond prior LLM-in-the-loop evolutionary pipelines by elevating the agent from candidate generator to variation operator, and can discover performance-critical micro-architectural optimizations that produce kernels surpassing state-of-the-art expert-engineered attention implementations on today's most advanced GPU hardware.

papers.title_en: Revisiting Tree Search for LLMs: Gumbel and Sequential Halving for Budget-Scalable Reasoning
papers.authors: Leonid Ugadiarov, Yuri Kuratov, Aleksandr Panov, Alexey Skrynnik
papers.evidence: 大模型推理时的树搜索优化
papers.tldr: 针对大语言模型在推理任务中使用AlphaZero式树搜索时出现的性能随预算增加而下降的“缩放失效”问题，本文提出了ReSCALE框架。该方法通过引入Gumbel采样和顺序减半（Sequential Halving）机制替代传统的Dirichlet噪声和PUCT选择，成功恢复了推理性能的单调增长。在GSM8K和Game24任务上，ReSCALE显著提升了高预算下的准确率，证明了其在预算可扩展推理中的有效性。
papers.abstract_en: Neural tree search is a powerful decision-making algorithm widely used in complex domains such as game playing and model-based reinforcement learning. Recent work has applied AlphaZero-style tree search to enhance the reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) during inference, but we find that this approach suffers from a scaling failure: on GSM8K and Game24, accuracy drops as the search budget increases. In this paper, we present ReSCALE, an adaptation of Gumbel AlphaZero MCTS that replaces Dirichlet noise and PUCT selection with Gumbel sampling and Sequential Halving, restoring monotonic scaling without changes to the model or its training. ReSCALE reaches 58.4\% on GSM8K and 85.3\% on Game24 at budgets where the baseline degrades. Ablations confirm that Sequential Halving is the primary driver of the improvement.
